summary: Large item-system decompilation pass for src/melee/it/items/it_2E5A.c. The PR replaced many it_2E5A stubs/prototypes with typed implementations, added item-specific variable and data-attribute structs, updated Item.xDD4_itemVar to include it_2E5A_ItemVars, and refined several shared item helper prototypes in it_2725.h. The author explicitly noted the result was only an 87.8% match and that the remaining difficulty involved weird structs, so this should be treated as a substantial but not fully solved matching/typing pass.

key_files.role: Main implementation file; grew from mostly stubs to ~700 added lines covering spawn/setup helpers, collision/bounce handling, state callbacks, accessory callback, and item teardown/access behavior.
Replaced many UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS declarations with typed prototypes for it_802E5AC4 through it_802E6ACC and included itCommonItems.h for shared structs.
Added it_2E5A_ItemVars_struct, it_2E5A_ItemVars, it_2E5A_DatAttrs_1, it_2E5A_DatAttrs_2, and it_802E5FXX_struct.
Refined shared item helper prototypes used by it_2E5A, including it_8027321C, it_80275640, it_80276D9C, and it_80277C40.
Added it_2E5A_ItemVars to the Item.xDD4_itemVar union.

what_changed: Implemented it_802E5AC4 as a bool collision/physics update routine using Item, CollData, ItemAttr, special attributes, mpColl_80048844, mpColl_8004CB78, velocity thresholding, floor-normal reflection, lbVector helpers, and landing-count logic.

Implemented spawn entry points it_802E5F00 and it_802E5F8C using a shared stack parameter struct, SpawnItem initialization, angle normalization around M_PI/M_PI_2, facing_dir selection, and gm_8016C6C0 for one parameter path.

Added it_802E609C as a loop that repeatedly prepares item spawning, calls Item_80268B18, applies it_802E61C4/it_802E6380/it_802E66A0, attaches debug ownership with db_80225DD8, and finalizes with it_802750F8.
Added inline spawn initialization helper it_802E614C to fill SpawnItem kind, position, velocity, parent gobjs, damage, and flags.
Added it_802E61C4 to compute a spawn/count value from special attributes, arg1, arg2, and HSD_Randf, clamping the result to at least 1.
Added it_802E628C to derive item velocity from random values, special attributes, facing direction, and trigonometric calculations.
Added it_802E6380 to select an attribute tier, write xDD4_itemVar.it_2E5A fields, set item lifetime/scale/D84/collision data, install joint/ECB data, and configure state/accessory behavior.
Added ItemStateTable it_803F8BD8 with three entries using it_802E67A8, it_802E67F4, it_802E6824, it_802E68EC, it_802E690C, and it_802E6910.
Added static zero Vec3 globals it_803B8718 and it_803B8724 used during velocity/collision vector calculations.
Converted it_2E5A.h declarations from broad UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS to typed signatures such as bool, void, s32, Item_GObj*, HSD_GObj*, Vec3*, SpawnItem*, and it_802E5FXX_struct*.

Updated it_2725.h helper prototypes from unknown signatures to typed forms needed by the implementation: void it_8027321C(Item_GObj*), void it_80275640(Item_GObj*, f32), bool it_80276D9C(Item_GObj*, s32), and void it_80277C40(Item_GObj*, s32).
Added it_2E5A_ItemVars to the Item union so code can address item->xDD4_itemVar.it_2E5A fields directly.
smart_moves: The PR localized ambiguous per-item state into it_2E5A_ItemVars instead of scattering raw xDD4 offsets through the C file.

The author preserved uncertainty in struct comments while still encoding observed field uses, such as fields passed to it_80273318, it_80275D5C, db_80225DD8, gm_8016C6C0, item->xD84, item->scl, and item->x40_vel.
Shared spawn parameters for it_802E5F00 and it_802E5F8C were factored into it_802E5FXX_struct, making the call pattern to it_802E609C/it_802E6380 explicit.
Replacing UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS with concrete prototypes in both the local item header and shared it_2725.h reduced type ambiguity for downstream matching work.

The implementation kept evidence comments for alternative interpretations, such as possible HSD_GObj vs s32 item-var fields and DatAttrs_1 vs DatAttrs_2 indexing, which is useful when the match is not complete.
The code used existing engine helpers for vector math, random values, item spawning, collision, and debug ownership instead of open-coding every operation.
decomp_lessons: For difficult item files, first make all caller/callee prototypes concrete; even partial typing can unlock large stub replacements.

When special-attribute tables appear to have multiple overlapping layouts, it can be useful to model separate candidate structs, as this PR did with it_2E5A_DatAttrs_1 and it_2E5A_DatAttrs_2, while preserving comments about uncertain indexing.
Item variable unions should get a named per-item arm once multiple xDD4 fields are accessed; this makes offset reasoning searchable and less error-prone.
A partial match can still be valuable if it documents field usage, register-class observations, and call-site evidence clearly.

For physics-heavy item behavior, matching may depend on exact math helper choice, including sqrtf_accurate vs sqrtf, M_PI constants, vector normalization/mirroring helpers, and small velocity threshold comparisons.
Stack-layout pressure remained visible through PAD_STACK and unused locals, so future work should revisit stack/register allocation rather than assuming the added C is final.
naming_conventions: Address-based symbol names were retained for unknown functions, e.g. it_802E5AC4, it_802E609C, it_802E6380, and it_803F8BD8.
Unknown struct fields use x-offset naming such as x0, x4, xDD4, xD84, x40_vel, and xC4_article_data.
Per-item data is named by item address prefix: it_2E5A_ItemVars, it_2E5A_DatAttrs_1, and it_2E5A_DatAttrs_2.
The helper struct name it_802E5FXX_struct explicitly signals a shared layout for nearby functions it_802E5F00 and it_802E5F8C.
Comments annotate inferred semantics without renaming fields prematurely, using notes like lifetime?, item->scl, item->xD84, angle?, timer?, and called in it_80273318.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used PAD_STACK and unused locals in several functions to influence stack layout and matching.
Kept commented alternative signatures and interpretations near difficult functions, showing active testing of parameter types such as Item_GObj*, HSD_GObj*, SpawnItem*, Vec3*, f32, f64, and s32.
Modeled a stack struct for spawn variables so assignments match the expected contiguous stack layout around it_802E5F00/it_802E5F8C.
Used explicit casts between Item_GObj* and HSD_GObj* around GET_ITEM and parent-gobj assignments to satisfy current type boundaries.
Used address-indexed attr access patterns like attr[var_r31 + 1] and attr[var_r31 + 2] to approximate special-attribute table layout where the exact struct was still uncertain.
Implemented exact-looking floating-point control flow with angle wrap loops, M_PI-derived constants, cosf, sqrtf/sqrtf_accurate, SQ, and 0.00001f/0.01f thresholds.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews are present in the provided PR slice.
The only human PR text says: "Only an 87.8% match, but not sure where to go from here. Lots of weird structs."
follow_up_queries: Revisit it_2E5A.c after later PRs for improved match percentage or renamed structs.
Search for later changes to it_2E5A_DatAttrs_1 and it_2E5A_DatAttrs_2 to see whether the special-attribute layout was resolved.
Search for later prototype refinements of it_8027321C, it_80275640, it_80276D9C, it_80277C40, mpColl_80048844, and mpColl_8004CB78.
Compare future versions of it_802E609C and it_802E6380 for stack-struct or attr-indexing fixes.
Look for follow-up work on Item.xDD4_itemVar.it_2E5A flags, especially UnkFlagStruct x18 bit assignments.
